source.title: Suppressing fear in the presence of a safety cue requires infralimbic cortical signaling to central amygdala

confidence_rationale: Circuit-level chemogenetic dissection showing IL->CeA (not IL->BLA) is the necessary projection for expressing learned safety in the presence of threat. This is downstream-specificity evidence for the IL node that MECH-303 centres on. The IL->BLA null finding is particularly clean: it rules out the simpler fear-extinction story (IL->BLA consolidation) and isolates a separate recall pathway. Transfer risk is moderate (rodent only, male only, single conditioning paradigm), but the circuit specificity is high.
failure_signatures: Female rats in this paradigm typically do not show fear suppression to the fear+safety cue, raising the possibility that the IL->CeA mechanism is sex-limited in this species and task. If MECH-303 is implemented as a species-general mechanism, the female data constitute a disconfirmation candidate.
The study tested only expression (recall), not acquisition, of safety. It is unclear whether IL->CeA is also required to initially encode the safety-cue prediction store that MECH-303 posits.
Safety was demonstrated via fear suppression, not via approach toward safety-associated stimuli. The approach-toward-safety arm of MECH-303 is not tested here.

mapping.source_claim_statement: The infralimbic cortex projection to the central amygdala is necessary for expressing learned fear suppression in the presence of a safety cue; the projection to the basolateral amygdala is not.
mapping.ree_translation: MECH-303 posits a prefrontal substrate (IL-equivalent) for safety-cue prediction. This paper localises the critical output pathway for safety-cue expression to IL->CeA, which in REE terms maps to the PFC-equivalent gating node that modulates the threat-salience stream. The IL->CeA circuit acts as a release valve on avoidance commitment -- exactly what MECH-303 calls commitment-release threshold modulation.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The paper measures fear suppression under conflicting cue compound (fear+safety simultaneously), not the standalone safety-cue prediction store. The IL->CeA result establishes the output path of safety expression, not the encoding store itself. MECH-303 is primarily a claim about the predictive store (what encodes 'this context predicts safety'), which may sit upstream of the IL->CeA output.
mapping.source_context: Male Long Evans rats, Pavlovian conditional discrimination task, cue-compound safety suppression, chemogenetic projection-specific inhibition.

confidence_components.notes: High methodological quality (intersectional chemogenetics, within-subjects design, histological verification, virus-free and miss controls). Mapping fidelity docked because the paper addresses the expression/output pathway rather than the predictive encoding store that is MECH-303's primary target. Transfer risk is moderate: rodent-male only and species-specific cue compounds.
